mnemonics: SLLC: Static Like Lego, Linked Like Chain
keyPoints: Static Allocation uses a fixed block of contiguous memory, allocated at compile time.
Linked Allocation uses non-contiguous memory blocks connected by pointers, allocated at runtime.
Static structures have fixed size and faster access, while linked structures are dynamic and flexible in size.
commonMistakes: Assuming static allocation can resize like dynamic allocation.
Forgetting that linked lists require extra memory for pointers.
Using static allocation for unpredictable data sizes, leading to wasted memory or overflow.
